content: Computer automation agents designed for routine task execution can be repurposed to serve as 'curiosity demonstration anchors' for ICM curiosity-driven autonomous agents, where the automation agent’s fixed, predictable action sequences create controlled, low-entropy contrast that amplifies the ICM agent’s intrinsic reward signal from exploring unknown state spaces. This contrast-driven curiosity boost lets ICM agents avoid the common stagnation trap of exploring only trivial, high-unpredictability noise instead of structurally novel useful environments.
